The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.

body:value: The data refers to salient features of Wankel Type Rotary Engine and Four Cylinder Two Stroke Reciprocating Engine. 55 hp Wankel type engine indigenously designed and developed in the country for the first time. The development of rotary engine project was initially originated from Vehicles Research and Development Establishment (VRDE), Ahmednagar, and jointly executed by NAL, a CSIR lab, and ADE (DRDO), Bangalore. The engine was cleared for flight after a rigorous ground and endurance test run. First ever-successful test-flight of an indigenous Rotary Engine- powering Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV), Nishant, was carried out at ADE, Bangalore. The flight took off at 1157 hrs in the morning of 31 Mar 2009 and reached an altitude of 1.8 Kms effortlessly before cruising for duration of 35 minutes.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: DRDO Product
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Defence
Department of Defence Research and Development
Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
field_sector:name: Defence
title: Rotary and Wankel type Engine

body:value: This data gives information about those states, which are able to claim the incentive provided by central government for purchase of land to provide home site to the IAY beneficiaries leading to additional target achieved under the program. As large poor population in country do not have the land to build IAY house for the benefit of BPL, only a few States such as Bihar, Karnataka, Kerala, Rajasthan, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, UP and Sikkim have requested for funds in 2009-10 and 2010-11 for purchase of homestead land under this scheme.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Development
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Urban
Rural
Development
title: Scheme For Purchase of Home Site And Incentive For Additional Target Under IAY - Twelfth Five Year Plan Document

body:value: The data refers to details of School and Teacher Indicators in urban areas. It gives information regarding percentage of Schools with PTR less than 40, Schools with Graduate Teachers, Schools with Multigrade classes, Female teachers, SC/ST teachers, Average number of teachers per school, Teacher Vacancy positions and teachers appointed under SSA. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: School and Teacher Indicators - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to some of the school level ratios. It provides details of percentage of schools with only male teachers, percentage of schools with PTR less than 40, percentage of multi-grade schools, percentage of single teacher schools, percentage of schools with graduate teachers, average number of teachers per school, teacher vacancies in schools (% of posts) and teachers recruited under SSA (% of total teachers). The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: School Indicators - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to the availability of schools by type of management in the selected villages and in the selected blocks. It provides details on the number of local body schools, government aided schools and private schools in the surveyed states. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Schools by Type of Management - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to information on Schools Covered under CMDM, MDM and Uncovered Schools in the Selected Sample States. It provides details such as Total Schools, Enrolment Schools Covered under CMDM, Schools Covered under MDM, No. of Uncovered Schools and Enrolment in the Uncovered Schools. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Schools Covered under CMDM, MDM and Uncovered Schools in the Selected Sample States - Based on Sample Survey
